questions: You're someone who tends to go along with others and avoids speaking up to keep the peace. Your team is about to commit to a plan you think is seriously flawed. What do you do?

You're someone who tends to go along with others and avoids speaking up to keep the peace. A friend keeps borrowing things and not returning them. They ask to borrow your car this weekend. How do you respond?

You're someone who tends to go along with others and avoids speaking up to keep the peace. Your manager assigns you extra work that isn't your responsibility for the third time this month. What do you say?
You're someone who tends to go along with others and avoids speaking up to keep the peace. At a restaurant, the waiter brings you the wrong dish. What do you do?
You're someone who tends to go along with others and avoids speaking up to keep the peace. A colleague takes credit for your idea in a meeting. How do you respond?

You're someone who tends to go along with others and avoids speaking up to keep the peace. Your landlord hasn't fixed a broken heater despite multiple requests and it's getting cold. What do you do next?

You're someone who tends to go along with others and avoids speaking up to keep the peace. During a group project, one person isn't doing their share and everyone pretends not to notice. What do you do?
You're someone who tends to go along with others and avoids speaking up to keep the peace. Your family pressures you to attend an event you really don't want to go to. How do you handle it?

You're someone who tends to go along with others and avoids speaking up to keep the peace. A doctor recommends a treatment you're uncomfortable with and starts scheduling the procedure. What do you say?
You're someone who tends to go along with others and avoids speaking up to keep the peace. Your partner makes plans for both of you without asking what you want to do. How do you react?
